title: Confusion-Geometric Supervised Contrastive Learning: Shaping Embedding Geometry from Training Dynamics
description: We propose CG-SupCon, which introduces a Confusion-Geometric Alignment (CGA) regularizer that prescribes a target embedding geometry derived from the model's online confusion matrix. Unlike prior work that modulates existing loss terms (temperature/weight scaling), CGA is a new optimization objective that directly drives class prototypes toward a confusion-aware configuration where confused pairs are more angularly separated. We prove that CG-SupCon's equilibrium geometry is uniquely determined by the confusion spectrum for general C classes, with inter-class distances monotonically ordered by confusion strength.
motivation: Standard SupCon converges to a symmetric simplex ETF where all class means are equidistant (Lee et al., AISTATS 2025), discarding meaningful inter-class structure and wasting gradient capacity on already-separated classes. Prior adaptive methods either require external hierarchies (LA-SCL), operate at the instance level (HardNeg-CL, TCL), use per-sample confidence (VarCon), or modulate existing loss terms incrementally (temperature/weight scaling). The gap: no method prescribes a target embedding geometry from learned confusion patterns. The model's confusion matrix encodes a rich, adaptive class similarity graph -- we use it to define and enforce a structured, non-ETF geometry.
proposed_approach: CG-SupCon combines standard SupCon with a novel Confusion-Geometric Alignment (CGA) regularizer. (1) Track a confusion matrix C via EMA of model predictions during training. (2) Maintain class prototypes as EMA of L2-normalized embeddings. (3) Define a target similarity matrix s*(i,j) = -1/(C-1) - alpha*(c_norm(i,j) - 1/(C-1)), prescribing larger angular separation for confused pairs. (4) The CGA loss penalizes deviations from this target: L_CGA = sum c(i,j)*(p_i.p_j - s*(i,j))^2. (5) Optionally add confusion-adaptive temperature in SupCon for instance-level complementarity. The key novelty is CGA: a geometric alignment regularizer (not a modulation of existing terms) that operates on class prototypes and has provable properties for general C.

related_work.title: Supervised Contrastive Learning
related_work.authors: Khosla, P., Teterwak, P., Wang, C., et al.
related_work.relationship: Foundation method. Converges to symmetric ETF. CG-SupCon adds CGA regularizer to break ETF toward confusion-structured geometry.

related_work.title: A Theoretical Framework for Preventing Class Collapse in Supervised Contrastive Learning
related_work.authors: Lee, C., Oh, J., Lee, K., & Sohn, J.
related_work.relationship: Proves SupCon converges to simplex ETF. Our Theorem 1 extends this: CGA regularizer breaks ETF symmetry for general C, with formal bounds.

related_work.title: Variational Supervised Contrastive Learning
related_work.authors: Wang, Z., Fan, J., Nguyen, T., Ji, H., & Liu, G.
related_work.relationship: Current SOTA (78.29% CIFAR-100 ResNet-50). Per-sample confidence-adaptive temperature via ELBO. CG-SupCon uses class-pair confusion for geometric alignment -- complementary signal. Fair comparison: we test VarCon's temperature mechanism and cite reported numbers.

related_work.title: Contrastive Learning with Hard Negative Samples
related_work.authors: Robinson, J., Chuang, C.-Y., Sra, S., & Jegelka, S.
related_work.relationship: Instance-level hard negative mining. CG-SupCon operates at class level via prototypes. Direct baseline for class-level vs instance-level comparison.

related_work.title: Tuned Contrastive Learning
related_work.relationship: Instance-level gradient tuning with learnable parameters. CG-SupCon uses class-pair-level geometric alignment. Direct baseline.

related_work.title: MACL: Multi-Label Adaptive Contrastive Learning Loss for Remote Sensing Image Retrieval
related_work.authors: Gao, Y., Chen, L., & Wang, P.
related_work.relationship: Uses pairwise label-overlap reweighting and Jaccard-based temperature for multi-label retrieval. CG-SupCon differs: (1) learned confusion vs static label overlap, (2) geometric alignment vs loss reweighting, (3) single-label classification vs multi-label retrieval. We adapt MACL-style reweighting as a baseline.

related_work.title: Dynamic Contrastive Learning Guided by Class Confidence and Confusion Degree for Medical Image Segmentation
related_work.authors: Chen, J., Chen, C., Huang, W., et al.
related_work.relationship: Pixel-level confusion for sampling in segmentation. CG-SupCon: class-level geometric regularizer for classification -- different task, signal granularity, and mechanism.

related_work.title: Prevalence of Neural Collapse during the Terminal Phase of Deep Learning Training
related_work.authors: Papyan, V., Han, X.Y., & Donoho, D.L.
related_work.relationship: Discovered ETF collapse. CG-SupCon's theory shows how to break ETF in a controlled, confusion-structured way.

related_work.title: Adaptive Margin Contrastive Learning for Ambiguity-aware 3D Semantic Segmentation
related_work.relationship: Per-point ambiguity-based margins for 3D segmentation. CG-SupCon: class-pair confusion-based geometric alignment for classification -- different signal, granularity, and mechanism.

related_work.title: Temperature Schedules for Self-Supervised Contrastive Methods
related_work.authors: Kukleva, A., Kuehne, H., Schiele, B., & Brox, T.
related_work.relationship: Global temperature scheduling. CG-SupCon's primary contribution (CGA) is a geometric regularizer, not a temperature schedule.

hypothesis: By adding a Confusion-Geometric Alignment (CGA) regularizer to SupCon that prescribes a confusion-aware target geometry for class prototypes, CG-SupCon will: (1) produce embeddings whose inter-class distances are monotonically ordered by confusion strength (provably breaking ETF symmetry), (2) improve classification accuracy with disproportionately larger gains on fine-grained distinctions, (3) outperform both instance-level methods and pairwise reweighting approaches, and (4) improve downstream tasks (few-shot, corruption robustness) where structured geometry has larger impact. The CGA regularizer alone (without temperature modulation) should provide the primary benefit, validating the geometric alignment as the core contribution.
success_criteria: CG-SupCon achieves statistically significant (p < 0.05, 5 seeds) improvement over SupCon on CIFAR-100 top-1 accuracy
CG-SupCon outperforms instance-level baselines (HardNeg-CL, TCL) on CIFAR-100
CG-SupCon outperforms pairwise reweighting (MACL-style) on CIFAR-100
CGA alone provides measurable gain over SupCon (primary contribution validated)
Within-superclass accuracy improvement exceeds between-superclass improvement
Inter-class distance matrix correlates more strongly with ground-truth hierarchy (Spearman rho improvement > 0.1)
Gains hold on at least one additional dataset beyond CIFAR-100

computational_requirements.tier1_core: 5.5 hours -- CIFAR-100 with all baselines (5 seeds), CGA ablation, alpha/lambda grid, overhead measurement
computational_requirements.tier2_important: 2.5 hours -- CIFAR-10, few-shot, CIFAR-100-C, stability analysis, Tiny-ImageNet, ImageNet-100, ResNet-50
computational_requirements.cutback_strategy: Reduce TCL/VarCon-T to 1 seed (saves ~0.4h), skip ImageNet-100 (saves ~0.7h). Core 5-seed CIFAR-100 comparison always preserved.
computational_requirements.cga_overhead: O(C^2) per step: ~640K multiply-adds for C=100, d=128 -- negligible (<5% of training time)
